tell us about a deliverable you produced -- an ssp section, a gap assessment, a poa&m, or similar -- that had to be significantly reworked. what happened, what did you do, and what was the actual consequence?
describe a time a client or stakeholder pushed back on a finding or a compliance position you took. what was the specific objection, how did you handle it, and what was the outcome?
which best describes what energizes you most about the way you work?
which of these sounds most like the kind of work you want to do?
what are you most hoping to get out of this role?
what is your availability to start a new role?
where do you see yourself in 5 to 10 years professionally?
ai_only: By providing your phone number you agree to receive informational text messages from Hotman Group.
reasons: field count delta 8 exceeds tolerance 2 (detector=39, ai=31)

ai.notes: The form uses a dynamic questionnaire system. Some fields are conditionally rendered or trigger disqualification logic based on user input.

fields.question: Upload Resume
Full Name
Email Address
Phone Number
By providing your phone number you agree to receive informational text messages from Hotman Group.
Desired Salary
Salary Period
Experience Summary
Cover Letter
Are you legally authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship, now or in the future?
Will you ever require H1B visa sponsorship to work in the United States?
What city and state are you currently located in?
This position is fully remote. Describe your current home workspace.
options: I have a private room with a door I can close, used exclusively or primarily as my workspace
I have a dedicated desk or area but the room is shared and I cannot always close a door
I do not have a dedicated workspace -- I work wherever I happen to be
I typically work from coffee shops, libraries, or shared spaces outside my home

fields.question: Which Microsoft 365 applications do you use regularly? Select all that apply.
What is your expected hourly rate for this contract engagement? (N/A, negotiable, market rate etc will result in disqualification. Please provide a number)
If this role converts to a permanent full-time position, what are your annual salary expectations? (N/A, negotiable, market rate etc will result in disqualification. Please provide a number)
What is the highest level of education you have completed?
Which of the following cybersecurity or GRC certifications do you currently hold? Select all that apply.
Are you currently bound by a non-compete, non-solicitation, or similar agreement that could restrict your ability to work for Hotman Group or its clients?
Do you consent to a background check as a condition of employment or contract engagement?
Are you willing to provide professional references?
Walk us through your CMMC and NIST experience. What engagements have you supported, what was your specific role, and what did you produce or deliver?
Which CMMC, NIST, or federal compliance frameworks have you worked with hands-on? For each one you list, describe the specific work you did -- not just that you have experience with it.

Describe your hands-on experience developing or maintaining System Security Plans. Walk us through a specific SSP you worked on -- what was the context, what was your role, what did you build or contribute, and what challenges came up in the process?
Describe your experience with CUI scoping. How have you approached boundary determinations in practice -- where do you start, what does the process look like, and what complications have come up?

Describe your hands-on experience with POA&Ms. What types of findings have you tracked, what did your POA&M documentation look like in practice, and how did you manage remediation progress with clients or stakeholders?
Why are you applying to this role, and why Hotman Group specifically?

A client asks you to mark several NIST SP 800-171 practices as implemented in their SSP because they plan to implement the controls within the next 90 days. They say their previous consultant did it this way. What do you do?
options: Mark them as implemented -- the client has confirmed their intent and 90 days is a reasonable window
Mark them as implemented with a note that implementation is planned -- that way the record reflects the situation
Explain that practices cannot be marked as implemented until they are actually in place, document the planned controls as part of the POA&M instead, and note the target dates there
Escalate to your engagement lead before responding since this involves a documentation integrity question

fields.question: You are mid-engagement and a client's IT team tells you that a system containing CUI was not included in the original scoping discussion. It is clearly within the CUI boundary. What do you do?
options: Stay focused on the original scope -- what was not included in scoping is not your responsibility on this engagement
Document what you found, flag it to your engagement lead immediately, and raise it with the client as a scoping gap that needs to be addressed
Address it yourself within your current engagement to make sure the client is protected
Mention it verbally to the client without documenting it or looping in your engagement lead

fields.question: A client wants to use a cloud service that is not FedRAMP authorized to store and process CUI. They say it is widely used in their industry and they have not had any issues. What do you do?
options: Document their preference and proceed -- it is their environment and their risk to accept

Explain the DFARS and CMMC requirements around CUI protection clearly, document your recommendation against it, and let them make an informed decision with full awareness of the compliance and contractual risk
Escalate to your engagement lead before responding since this involves a significant compliance risk
Flag it as a low-priority observation since the client has assessed the risk themselves

fields.question: You have been on an engagement for a month and feel like you are not being given enough responsibility. The work assigned to you feels below your capability. What do you do?
options: Keep delivering on what is in front of you and trust that more responsibility will follow as you demonstrate your work
Identify what you would need to demonstrate to take on more and start building toward it
